candidates.evidence_quote: U.S. combined and Outback Steakhouse comparable restaurant sales of (1.1)% and (1.2)%, respectively
U.S. combined and Outback Steakhouse comparable restaurant sales of (1.1)% and (1.2)%, respectively
We regularly remodel restaurants across all of our concepts to maintain the relevance of our restaurants’ ambience, focused on driving additional traffic to our restaurants.
We completed 65 restaurant remodels during 2024 and more than 100 restaurant remodels during 2023.
Our U.S. loyalty program, Dine Rewards, is designed to drive incremental traffic and provide data for customer segmentation and personalization opportunities.
Beef represents the majority of purchased proteins in the U.S.

The cost of such commodities may fluctuate widely due to government policy and regulation, changing weather patterns and conditions, climate change and other supply and/or demand impacting events such as pandemics, macroeconomic conditions, geopolitical events or other unforeseen circumstances.
Other major commodity categories purchased include seafood, pork, poultry, produce, dairy, bread, oils and pasta and energy sources to operate our restaurants, such as natural gas and electricity.

In 2024, our U.S. restaurants purchased beef raw materials primarily from four beef suppliers. Due to the nature of our industry, we expect to continue purchasing a substantial amount of beef from a small number of suppliers.
We opened 14 Outback Steakhouse restaurants during 2024 and plan to open approximately 15 additional locations in 2025.

In recent years, we began developing our U.S. Outback Steakhouse restaurants utilizing a smaller-scaled “Joey” design. The Joey was designed to increase return on investment through a reduced restaurant footprint with a more efficient layout.

We continue to pursue international expansion opportunities through our franchise partners, leveraging established franchised markets in South America, Asia and the Middle East, with a focus on Brazil, through our minority interest in these operations.

On December 30, 2024, we completed the sale of 67% of our Brazil operations (the “Brazil Sale Transaction”) and entered into amended and restated franchise agreements with all existing restaurants in Brazil.
Under our franchise agreements, each franchisee is required to pay an initial franchise fee and monthly royalties based on a percentage of gross restaurant sales.
To increase customer convenience, we leverage our online ordering infrastructure to facilitate off-premises dining systems.
During 2024, our turnover rates for U.S. hourly restaurant Team Members and U.S. restaurant management were 84% and 20%, respectively.

At an aggregate level, all major casual dining restaurants in markets in which we operate would be considered competitors of our concepts. We also face growing competition from the supermarket industry which offers expanded selections of prepared meals.
Alcoholic beverage sales represent 11% of our U.S. restaurant sales.
Severe storms, extended periods of inclement weather or climate change may affect the seasonal operating results of the areas impacted.

We utilize both an internal food safety team responsible for supplier evaluations and external third parties who inspect supplier adherence and restaurant practices to monitor quality, food safety and product specifications.
We focus on data segmentation and personalization, customer relationship management and digital advertising to be more efficient and relevant with our advertising expenditures.
Operating income and restaurant-level operating margins of 3.5% and 13.3%, respectively, as compared to 6.8% and 15.4%, respectively for 2023
Operating income and restaurant-level operating margins of 3.5% and 13.3%, respectively, as compared to 6.8% and 15.4%, respectively for 2023

notes: Chunk contains a single event: 10-K part 1 of 5 (Item 1 Business plus the start of Item 7 MD&A overview); no fiscal_kpis present in this chunk.

Skipped (vague/banned/instance-bound): online-ordering tech investment (covered by off_premises_sales and loyalty_program), Out West advertising-fee concession (franchisee legal name + single instance), minimum-wage/labor regulation boilerplate list, 'negative economic conditions' (bare macro), seasonality pattern, cybersecurity boilerplate pointer, diluted EPS YoY result and total revenue YoY decline (outcome metrics with no surprise/guidance context), trademark/IP boilerplate.

operating_margin and restaurant_level_operating_margin share one evidence sentence but are distinct margin levels (R2); same_store_sales vs outback_steakhouse_same_store_sales kept separate per the brand-metric rule (R9).
